leaderRationale: Parse failed: ERROR: codex failed: spawn codex ENOENT

conceptual_groups.summary: Canonical output paths are unresolved across two consecutive rounds — <outputs> section omits the required directory prefix for promoted artifacts

findings.description: All four canonical artifact entries in <outputs> use bare slug-relative names (e.g. '<slug>-role.md') without the canonical directory prefix 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/'. The self-check raised MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT for all four paths in round 0 and again in round 1 with zero improvements. The freeze condition cannot be verified without knowing where on-disk promoted artifacts land.

Step 2 draft outputs use 'drafts/<slug>-role.md' path prefix, but <outputs> lists the canonical entries without any path prefix at all. The promotion path from drafts/ to canonical is therefore implicit and unverifiable from the role definition alone.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: In the <outputs> section, qualify every canonical artifact path with its target directory (e.g. 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/<slug>-role.md'). Add a one-line 'promoted to:' annotation under each canonical entry so the self-check and any downstream validator can resolve the path without inference.

conceptual_groups.summary: Revision loop stalled — two consecutive rounds of leader pushback with zero improvements and no arbitration triggered

findings.description: Step 4 specifies 'Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits', but two full rounds have completed with 0 improvements and the arbitration path was never entered. The role's own governance rule was not enforced, creating a contradictory execution trace.

The role does not define what distinguishes a 'split' from consecutive leader-only pushback, so the arbitration trigger is ambiguous when the reviewer pair and leader disagree on separate axes rather than on the same finding.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Tighten the arbitration trigger: define 'consecutive splits' to include rounds where the leader reports unresolved material issues AND no net improvement in the finding count. Add a hard rule: if round N and round N+1 both close with unresolved_count > 0 and improvement_count == 0, arbitration is mandatory before round N+2 may begin.

conceptual_groups.summary: Duplicate 'Application workflow creation' entry in <scope> not-in-scope list

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice in the not-in-scope list — once bare and once with a parenthetical clarification. The duplication introduces noise and could cause a self-check to flag inconsistency.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Merge the two entries into the single annotated form: 'Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)'.

residual_risks: Even after paths are corrected, the self-check validator must be re-run to confirm all four MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT findings clear — approving on a manual assertion alone would leave unverified evidence.
The arbitration rule gap (f4) means that if a future run hits consecutive pushback without a paired-reviewer split, the trigger condition may again be missed.
The contract's 'max_review_rounds: 3' is not surfaced in the role's <guardrails> or <steps>, so the ceiling is invisible to reviewers reading only the markdown.
strengths: All nine required XML tags are present and correctly ordered.
Authority owns/does-not-own boundaries are clearly delineated with no overlap against llm-tool-builder or COO controller.
Freeze condition in <guardrails> is unambiguous: every reviewer must approve and the leader must report no unresolved material issues.
Evidence artifact taxonomy (canonical vs evidence vs internal) is well-structured and maps cleanly to Step 5 promotion logic.
Provenance and decision-history preservation requirements are explicit in <guardrails>.
